name: Ananya Rao
title: Senior Frontend Engineer @ Stripe
strengths: Led the design system rebuild at her current company, adopted by 40+ engineers
Deep TypeScript expertise with strong testing discipline
Strong written communication evident in RFC samples
weaknesses: Limited exposure to data visualization libraries
No direct experience with Tailwind v4
interviewQuestions: Walk us through a time you had to deprecate a widely-used component — how did you manage the migration?
How do you decide what belongs in a shared design token system versus a one-off override?
Describe a disagreement with a designer over an interaction pattern and how it resolved.
summary: Ananya is a strong match on every weighted criterion, with standout design-system leadership experience directly relevant to RedRank's component library work.

name: Devika Menon
title: Frontend Engineer @ Razorpay
strengths: Excellent performance optimization track record on large React apps
weaknesses: Has not owned a design system end-to-end, only consumed one
interviewQuestions: Tell us about the largest performance bottleneck you've diagnosed and fixed.
How would you approach building a component library from zero?
summary: Devika is a strong technical performer with excellent fundamentals, though slightly lighter on design-system ownership than top-ranked candidates.

name: Karthik Subramanian
title: Full Stack Engineer @ Freshworks
strengths: Broad full-stack experience, comfortable across the whole pipeline
weaknesses: Frontend-specific depth is thinner than dedicated frontend candidates
No evidence of design system or component library work
interviewQuestions: Of your full-stack background, what percentage of recent work has been frontend-focused?
How comfortable are you specializing primarily in frontend going forward?
summary: Karthik brings strong overall experience but lighter frontend specialization relative to the rubric's weighting toward React/TS and design systems.
notes: Following up to confirm willingness to specialize in frontend.

name: Priya Iyer
title: UI Engineer @ Zoho
strengths: Solid UI craftsmanship and attention to visual detail
Fast learner based on reference feedback
weaknesses: Only 3 years of experience, below the rubric's target
interviewQuestions: Describe the most complex TypeScript type you've had to model and why.
How do you approach ramping up on an unfamiliar large codebase?
summary: Priya shows promising craft but currently falls short on the experience and TypeScript-depth criteria the rubric weights most heavily.

name: Rohan Verma
title: Senior Software Engineer @ Atlassian
strengths: 8 years of experience across multiple enterprise SaaS products
Has mentored junior engineers and led technical design reviews
weaknesses: Most recent design-system work was as a contributor, not an owner
interviewQuestions: What's a design decision you pushed back on and how did you make your case?
How do you balance shipping speed with long-term component maintainability?
summary: Rohan is a well-rounded senior candidate with strong experience depth, slightly behind Ananya only on direct design-system ownership.
notes: Great culture-fit signal from reference call.

name: Meera Nair
title: Frontend Engineer @ Swiggy
strengths: Shipped several high-traffic consumer features under tight deadlines
Well regarded by cross-functional partners in reference checks
weaknesses: Limited exposure to formal design-system tooling
Test coverage on past projects has been inconsistent
interviewQuestions: Walk through how you'd introduce testing discipline to a team without one.
What's your experience contributing to or consuming a component library?
summary: Meera is a solid mid-to-senior candidate with strong delivery speed, though design-systems and testing depth trail the top of the pool.

name: Arjun Malhotra
title: Software Engineer @ Infosys
strengths: Non-CS-background candidate with demonstrated self-directed learning
weaknesses: No exposure to design systems or component libraries
interviewQuestions: Walk us through the most complex React component you've built end-to-end.
How have you approached learning TypeScript so far?
summary: Arjun doesn't yet meet the core technical bar this rubric weights most heavily, particularly around React/TypeScript depth and design-systems exposure.
